summary: preinstall script fetches remote code from non-publisher host and collects system info
details: dist/scripts/preinstall.js runs at npm install time and performs HTTPS GETs to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name (a non-publisher-owned, non-registry host with no apparent relationship to a package named '@gusmano/reext'). The script imports fs, http, https, and os; calls os.userInfo() and os.networkInterfaces(); reads process.env; and checks/writes filesystem paths via fs.existsSync. This is the canonical install-time external-fetch + host enumeration shape: a lifecycle hook pulling content from an opaque external endpoint while also gathering identifying information about the installer machine. There is no indication the endpoint is a legitimate publisher CDN, a pinned native-addon release, or a known runtime mirror (Chromium/Electron/etc.), and no hash/signature verification is visible. Any developer or CI runner executing `npm install @gusmano/reext` will contact that host and execute whatever behavior the preinstall dictates based on its response. Cited evidence: fs/https/http/os imports at dist/scripts/preinstall.js L3-7, https.get to 2tak.l.serverhost.name at L78-80 and L192-194, os.userInfo()/os.networkInterfaces() at L7/L227, process.env access at L17.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
